field_definitions.uber_status: Uber's own operating status in this country: nationwide | city_only | taxi_only | banned | absent | gray_area
field_definitions.apps: Ranked list of all rideshare apps available, most prominent first, each with its own status field
field_definitions.geographic_limitations: Specific cities/regions where service is available, if not nationwide
field_definitions.airport_pickup: Rules/restrictions for ride-hail pickup at the country's main international airport(s)
field_definitions.local_taxi_cash_requirement: Whether traditional taxis dominate and/or cash is required or preferred
field_definitions.volatile: Boolean - true if legal status is contested, disputed, actively changing, or inconsistently reported between sources
field_definitions.regulatory_alert: Boolean - true for countries with fast-moving 2024-2026 regulatory action (bans, suspensions, launches, litigation)
field_definitions.confidence: high | low - low indicates thin/hard-to-verify data (small islands, conflict zones, etc), flagged for manual verification
field_definitions.traveler_notes: SIM card, payment method, or other practical quirks for travelers
field_definitions.last_verified: Date this record was last checked against sources

advice_thresholds.absent_or_banned: If Uber shows no service, switch to the regional/local champion app (e.g. DiDi in China, Yandex Go in Russia, Snapp in Iran, Grab in most of SE Asia, local taxi app in the Bahamas).
advice_thresholds.taxi_only: Expect regulated taxi-meter fares, not discounted gig-economy pricing. Budget accordingly; the app is a dispatch convenience, not a price advantage.
advice_thresholds.gray_area: The app likely works in practice but may be barred from some airport pickup zones or contested by local taxi unions. Confirm airport-specific rules before relying on it, and consider sitting in the front seat as locals often do to reduce driver friction with authorities.
advice_thresholds.volatile_true: Status has changed recently or is actively disputed. Re-verify within days of travel, not months.
advice_thresholds.confidence_low: Data is thin or hard to verify (small islands, conflict zones, isolated states). Treat as directional, not definitive; a local taxi backup is recommended.

countries.traveler_notes: Yango withdrew 2024 over regulatory issues; use Yassir as primary.

countries.other_notes: Security constraints nationwide.
No verified ride-hailing app; traditional taxis only.
Government ordered suspension; Yango disputes and continues operating.
Traditional taxis only; security risk; data unavailable beyond taxi-only.

countries.airport_pickup: Uber/Careem allowed at Cairo airport (designated zone)
countries.other_notes: One of Africa's most mature ride-hailing markets.
Uber absent. Rich local app ecosystem; past regulatory threats to the RIDE model.
M-Pesa mobile money integration is key to local usage.
Security/political instability.

countries.geographic_limitations: Casablanca, Rabat, Marrakech (Uber); broader for inDrive
countries.other_notes: Ministry of Transport declared app ride-hailing outside the legal framework Oct 2025 (unlicensed transport punishable by fines/imprisonment); Uber nonetheless returned Nov 2025 — contradictory, most-cited volatile case globally. ~77,200 taxis and ~180,000 drivers nationally; 1,500+ professional licenses revoked in 2025.

countries.airport_pickup: FAAN parking-fee disputes at Lagos/Abuja airports
countries.other_notes: State-vs-federal regulation fights; ongoing airport-access disputes (₦45,000 FAAN parking fee).
Taxi unions have sued Yango/Yassir/Heetch; new registration rules pending.
Security constraints.

Yango suspended Oct 2024 (cited operating without authorization/safety).

Bolt suspended March 2025 (12M dinar/~$3.8M asset seizure over alleged tax evasion, money laundering, unlicensed operation) and ceased operations May 9 2025. Uber never entered market. State-backed fare-capping app introduced.

Uber and Grab both absent.

countries.other_notes: Uber sold its China business to DiDi in 2016 and does not operate. Foreign visitors need Alipay/WeChat or Amap's foreigner-friendly option.
Uber operates in a long-running legal gray zone; regulatory framework has been pending for years.
Rapido reported ~74M MAU by Feb 2026 vs Uber ~38M and Ola ~26-27M (India Dispatch/Moneycontrol).
Ojek (motorbike taxi) culture central to Gojek's model.
No Western apps due to sanctions. Snapp hit a record 5,896,797 daily trips in Feb 2025 per bne IntelliNews. Requires Iranian SIM to register.
Security considerations.
Private ridesharing liberalization is ongoing but still limited/regional as of 2025-2026; Uber functions mainly as a taxi-dispatch app.
inDrive's country of origin.
Economic crisis affects pricing/reliability.
